reasoning: The SONIA value on 2025-11-21 was 3.9690, which is the lowest value observed in November 2025 (most days were around 3.9694). Because 3.969 is a local minimum, any day where the Bank of England (BoE) maintains the current interest rate has a structurally high chance (~70%) of printing a SONIA rate slightly higher than 3.9690 due to normal fractional fluctuations. For November 30 (Nov 28 trading day), there are no intervening BoE meetings, so the rate is extremely likely to remain around the 3.969X level, giving a strong probability of a technical increase. For December 23, the BoE meets earlier in the month, but December is traditionally a non-Monetary Policy Report (MPR) month; having just held rates in November (an MPR month), the BoE is quite likely to hold again, keeping the baseline elevated. For February 21, 2026, the probability of resting above 3.9690 drops, as February is an MPR month and presents a significantly higher cumulative chance of a 25 bps rate cut (which would pull SONIA down to ~3.72%). In line with instructions, probabilities revert toward 0.5 over longer horizons due to macroeconomic uncertainty regarding terminal rates.
